gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An immutable persistent key→value map (value-semantic; spec §3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Iteration order is **insertion order** (the first insertion of each key determines its"] # [doc = " position). Duplicate-key inserts update the value but preserve the key's original"] # [doc = " insertion position. This order is a documented, stable property — never an exposed"] # [doc = " hash-bucket order (the no-silent-reorder crux, RFC-0016 §4.4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG: §7-Q1"] # [doc = " The ordered-vs-bucketed default is M-501's to ratify (spec §7-Q1). This implementation"] # [doc = " chooses *insertion order* as the deterministic floor. If M-501 ratifies a different"] # [doc = " order, this impl changes; the guarantee-matrix property test (order stability) will"] # [doc = " catch any silent regression."] # [derive (Debug , Clone)] pub struct Map < K , V > { # [doc = " Key-value pairs in insertion order (preserves documented iteration order)."] # [doc = " An `IndexMap`-like structure is appropriate here; we use a `Vec` + `HashMap`"] # [doc = " to avoid external dependencies while keeping insertion order stable."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Invariant: `order` and `index` are always in sync."] order : Vec < (K , V) > , # [doc = " Key → index in `order` (for O(n) lookup with no external deps)."] # [doc = " The hash is a *non-identity* bucketing hash (not `std.content`'s digest)."] index : HashMap < K , usize > , }
gaps.reason: struct `Map` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl < K : Clone + Eq + std :: hash :: Hash , V : PartialEq + Clone > PartialEq for Map < K , V > { fn eq (& self , other : & Self) -> bool { self . order == other . order } }

gaps.snippet: impl < K : Clone + Eq + std :: hash :: Hash , V : Clone > Map < K , V > { # [doc = " An empty `Map`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn empty () -> Self { Map { order : Vec :: new () , index : HashMap :: new () , } } # [doc = " The number of key→value pairs."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn len (& self) -> usize { self . order . len () } # [doc = " `true` iff the map has no entries."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn is_empty (& self) -> bool { self . order . is_empty () } # [doc = " The value for key `k`, or `None` when `k` is absent (C1 — never a default)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`. Fallibility: `None` on missing key."] # [must_use] pub fn get (& self , k : & K) -> Option < & V > { self . index . get (k) . map (| & i | & self . order [i] . 1) } # [doc = " `true` iff `k` is present."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn contains_key (& self , k : & K) -> bool { self . index . contains_key (k) } # [doc = " The value for key `k`, or `default` when `k` is absent."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The default is an **explicit named argument** — it is never a hidden fallback"] # [doc = " (C1). Callers who want absence to be explicit should use `get` (which returns"] # [doc = " `None`). `get_or` is the sanctioned \"I know the default\" surface."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN (C3)"] # [doc = " The default argument is reified at the call site: the caller can inspect"] # [doc = " `default` directly (no hidden fallback). This is the \"default is a NAMED arg,"] # [doc = " not silent\" guarantee from spec §3."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn get_or < 'a > (& 'a self , k : & K , default : & 'a V) -> & 'a V { self . get (k) . unwrap_or (default) } # [doc = " Insert `k → v`, returning a **new** `Map`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " If `k` is already present, the value is replaced (the key retains its original"] # [doc = " insertion position in the iteration order). The receiver is not modified (C4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn insert (& self , k : K , v : V) -> Self { let mut new_map = self . clone () ; if let Some (& i) = new_map . index . get (& k) { new_map . order [i] . 1 = v ; } else { let i = new_map . order . len () ; new_map . order . push ((k . clone () , v)) ; new_map . index . insert (k , i) ; } new_map } # [doc = " Remove `k`, returning `(new_map, Option<V>)`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The second component is `Some(v)` when `k` was present, `None` when absent"] # [doc = " (C1 — the absence is explicit, never silent). The returned `Map` is always"] # [doc = " a *new* value (C4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] pub fn remove (& self , k : & K) -> (Self , Option < V >) { if let Some (& i) = self . index . get (k) { let removed_v = self . order [i] . 1 . clone () ; let mut new_order : Vec < (K , V) > = Vec :: with_capacity (self . order . len () - 1) ; let mut new_index : HashMap < K , usize > = HashMap :: with_capacity (self . order . len () - 1) ; for (j , (ek , ev)) in self . order . iter () . enumerate () { if j != i { let new_i = new_order . len () ; new_order . push ((ek . clone () , ev . clone ())) ; new_index . insert (ek . clone () , new_i) ; } } (Map { order : new_order , index : new_index , } , Some (removed_v) ,) } else { (self . clone () , None) } } # [doc = " All keys in **insertion order** (documented — the honesty crux)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total. EXPLAIN: the documented insertion order is the"] # [doc = " inspectable artifact."] # [must_use] pub fn keys (& self) -> Vec < & K > { self . order . iter () . map (| (k , _) | k) . collect () } # [doc = " All values in **insertion order** (same documented order as `keys`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn values (& self) -> Vec < & V > { self . order . iter () . map (| (_ , v) | v) . collect () } # [doc = " All `(key, value)` pairs in **insertion order** (documented order)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total. EXPLAIN: insertion order is the inspectable artifact."] # [must_use] pub fn entries (& self) -> Vec < (& K , & V) > { self . order . iter () . map (| (k , v) | (k , v)) . collect () } }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; fn map_abc () -> Map < & 'static str , i32 > { Map :: empty () . insert ("a" , 1) . insert ("b" , 2) . insert ("c" , 3) } # [doc = " `insert` returns a new `Map`; original is unchanged (C4)."] # [test] fn insert_returns_new_map_original_unchanged () { let m = Map :: empty () ; let m2 = m . insert ("k" , 1) ; assert ! (m . is_empty () , "original must be unchanged after insert (C4)") ; assert_eq ! (m2 . len () , 1) ; } # [doc = " `remove` returns a new `Map`; original is unchanged (C4)."] # [test] fn remove_returns_new_map_original_unchanged () { let m = map_abc () ; let (m2 , v) = m . remove (& "b") ; assert_eq ! (m . len () , 3 , "original must be unchanged after remove (C4)") ; assert_eq ! (m2 . len () , 2) ; assert_eq ! (v , Some (2)) ; } # [doc = " `get` returns `None` on missing key — never a default (C1)."] # [test] fn get_returns_none_on_missing_key () { let m = map_abc () ; assert_eq ! (m . get (& "z") , None) ; } # [doc = " `remove` returns `None` in second position when key absent (C1)."] # [test] fn remove_returns_none_when_key_absent () { let m = map_abc () ; let (m2 , v) = m . remove (& "z") ; assert_eq ! (v , None , "C1: absence must be explicit (None), not silent") ; assert_eq ! (m2 . len () , 3 , "absent remove must return same-contents map") ; } # [doc = " `get_or` requires an explicit default (never a hidden fallback — C1)."] # [test] fn get_or_uses_explicit_default () { let m = map_abc () ; let def = 99 ; assert_eq ! (* m . get_or (& "z" , & def) , 99 , "missing key → explicit default") ; assert_eq ! (* m . get_or (& "a" , & def) , 1 , "present key → its value, not default") ; } # [doc = " `keys()`, `values()`, `entries()` all walk in insertion order."] # [test] fn keys_values_entries_walk_in_insertion_order () { let m = map_abc () ; assert_eq ! (m . keys () , vec ! [& "a" , & "b" , & "c"]) ; assert_eq ! (m . values () , vec ! [& 1 , & 2 , & 3]) ; assert_eq ! (m . entries () , vec ! [(& "a" , & 1) , (& "b" , & 2) , (& "c" , & 3)]) ; } # [doc = " Insert order determines position, not key hash (no silent reorder)."] # [test] fn insertion_order_preserved_regardless_of_key_hash () { let m = Map :: empty () . insert ("c" , 3) . insert ("b" , 2) . insert ("a" , 1) ; assert_eq ! (m . keys () , vec ! [& "c" , & "b" , & "a"] , "keys must walk in INSERTION order, not hash/alpha order (no silent reorder)") ; } # [doc = " Duplicate-key insert updates value but preserves original insertion position."] # [test] fn duplicate_insert_updates_value_preserves_position () { let m = map_abc () . insert ("b" , 99) ; assert_eq ! (m . keys () , vec ! [& "a" , & "b" , & "c"]) ; assert_eq ! (m . get (& "b") , Some (& 99)) ; } # [doc = " Property: two maps with identical contents (same keys+values) inserted in the same"] # [doc = " order yield the same `entries()` sequence."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guard: any non-determinism in insert makes this fail."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Spec §4 §7-Q2: this invariant is `Declared` (spec-phase); this test promotes it"] # [doc = " to `Empirical` by property-checking it for a representative sample. We do not claim"] # [doc = " `Proven` — no theorem is cited (VR-5)."] # [test] fn equal_contents_same_order_yield_same_entries_order () { let m1 = Map :: empty () . insert (1 , "x") . insert (2 , "y") . insert (3 , "z") ; let m2 = Map :: empty () . insert (1 , "x") . insert (2 , "y") . insert (3 , "z") ; assert_eq ! (m1 . entries () , m2 . entries () , "same contents + same insertion order must yield same entries() sequence (no silent reorder)") ; } # [doc = " Property: after a remove, the remaining keys keep their relative insertion order."] # [doc = " Guard: a remove that scrambles the remaining order makes this fail."] # [test] fn remove_preserves_relative_order_of_remaining_keys () { let m = map_abc () ; let (m2 , _) = m . remove (& "b") ; assert_eq ! (m2 . keys () , vec ! [& "a" , & "c"] , "remove must preserve relative insertion order of remaining keys (no silent reorder)") ; } # [doc = " Property: insert then remove round-trips (key absent after removal)."] # [test] fn insert_remove_round_trip () { let m = map_abc () ; let m2 = m . insert ("d" , 4) ; assert_eq ! (m2 . get (& "d") , Some (& 4)) ; let (m3 , v) = m2 . remove (& "d") ; assert_eq ! (v , Some (4)) ; assert_eq ! (m3 . get (& "d") , None) ; assert_eq ! (m3 . len () , m . len () , "round-trip must restore original length") ; } }
